titleStmt.title: Letter from Fellow & Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to Alfred Miller, 3 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland to Alfred Miller (1884-1942) concerning the committee's recommendation to restrict recognition of courses of lectures delivered at the Technical Schools and at the Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland.
Alfred Miller served as both the registrar of the Royal College of Surgeons (1910-1938) and as secretary to the Conjoint Board of the College of Physicians and College of Surgeons. At the outbreak of the Rising Miller instructed James Duncan, the college porter, to lock the college doors in order to deter Michael Mallin's occupying rebel force in St. Stephen's Green.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10th JULY 16. A. Miller, Esq.,
Secretary,
Committee of Management, Dublin. Dear Sir, In reply to your letter of the 5th inst., I am
requested by the President and Fellows of the Royal
College of Physicians of Ireland to state that they
have adopted the recommendation. of the Committee of
Management to restrict recognition of courses of
Lectures delivered at the Technical Schools and at
the Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland, to such Courses
only as are given by day. I remain,
Yours faithfully,
Fellow & Registrar.
